questions.q: Which deity's personal weapon was the Narayanastra?
options: Vishnu
Shiva
Brahma
Indra
questions.explanation: Narayana is a name of Vishnu, and the Narayanastra was his own astra, releasable only once in a lifetime.

questions.q: Who hurled the Narayanastra at the Pandava army during the Kurukshetra war?
options: Karna
Duryodhana
Ashwatthama
Kripacharya
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama unleashed the Narayanastra in fury after learning how his father Drona had been killed.

questions.q: What event drove Ashwatthama to unleash the Narayanastra?
options: The death of Duryodhana
The killing of his father Drona
The fall of Bhishma
The insult to Draupadi
questions.explanation: Drona was beheaded by Dhrishtadyumna after laying down his arms, and his grieving son answered with Vishnu's astra.

questions.q: How many times could a warrior use the Narayanastra in his lifetime?
options: Three times
Seven times
Twice
Only once
questions.explanation: The Narayanastra could be released only once in a lifetime; a second attempt would turn on the user's own side.

questions.q: Who told the Pandava army how to survive the Narayanastra?
options: Arjuna
Yudhishthira
Krishna
Vyasa
questions.explanation: Krishna alone knew the weapon's nature and commanded the entire army to disarm and surrender.

questions.q: What was the only sure defense against the Narayanastra?
options: Laying down all weapons and giving up even the will to fight
Hiding behind a wall of war elephants
Countering it with the Brahmastra
Diving beneath the waters of a river
questions.explanation: The astra spared only those who dismounted, dropped their weapons, and abandoned all thought of resistance.

questions.q: What did the Narayanastra rain down upon the Pandava host?
options: A poison mist that stole men's sight
A flood that swept across the field
A swarm of celestial serpents
Countless blazing missiles of every kind
questions.explanation: The weapon filled the sky with thousands of fiery arrows, discs, and maces that sought out every fighting man.

questions.q: During which conflict was the Narayanastra unleashed?
options: The siege of Mathura
The Kurukshetra war
The burning of Khandava
The raid on Virata's cattle
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama released it on the field of Kurukshetra after his father's death in the great war.

questions.q: What happened when a warrior tried to resist the Narayanastra?
options: It vanished and returned to its caster
It blazed all the fiercer against him
It split into two slower weapons
It turned aside to the nearest chariot
questions.explanation: The weapon's defining trait was that its fury grew in proportion to the resistance offered against it.

questions.q: From whom did Ashwatthama receive the Narayanastra?
options: Parashurama, his father's guru
Shiva, after fierce penance
Vyasa, at the war's beginning
His father Drona
questions.explanation: Drona possessed the secret of the Narayanastra and passed it to his son Ashwatthama.

questions.q: Which Pandava refused to lay down his weapons before the Narayanastra?
options: Bhima
Arjuna
Nakula
Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: Bhima alone kept fighting, declaring he would never disarm, and the astra concentrated its fire upon him.

questions.q: How was Bhima finally saved from the blazing Narayanastra?
options: He countered it with his whirling mace
Hanuman descended from the banner to shield him
Krishna and Arjuna dragged him down and forced his weapons from him
Drona's spirit interceded on his behalf
questions.explanation: Krishna and Arjuna leapt to Bhima, pulled him from his chariot, and stripped away his weapons so the astra would subside.

questions.q: Why did Ashwatthama refuse Duryodhana's plea to launch the Narayanastra a second time?
options: He had promised Drona to spare the Pandavas
The mantra needed a full day of preparation
Vyasa appeared and forbade him to speak it
Used twice, it would destroy the user's own side
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama explained that the weapon, invoked again, would turn upon the army of the one who released it.

questions.q: On which day of the Kurukshetra war was the Narayanastra unleashed?
options: The tenth day
The fifteenth day
The seventeenth day
The eighteenth day
questions.explanation: It was released on the fifteenth day, the same day Drona fell to Dhrishtadyumna's sword.

questions.q: Who explained to Ashwatthama why the Narayanastra had failed against the Pandavas?
options: Narada
Kripacharya
Vyasa
Sanjaya
questions.explanation: Vyasa appeared before the despairing Ashwatthama and revealed the divine identities of Krishna and Arjuna.

questions.q: How had Drona originally obtained the Narayanastra?
options: It was granted by Narayana himself, pleased with his devotion
He won it from Parashurama in a contest of arms
Bharadwaja stole it from Indra's armory
It rose from a sacrificial fire at Kampilya
questions.explanation: The Drona Parva relates that Narayana granted the astra to Drona, who later confided its secret to his son.

questions.q: What warning accompanied the gift of the Narayanastra to Ashwatthama?
options: It must never be used in haste or against those who refuse to fight
It could be fired only at sunrise while facing east
It could be aimed at only a single warrior at a time
It would fail if loosed upon Kurukshetra's holy soil
questions.explanation: Drona cautioned that the weapon must not be hurled rashly, for it would not slay those who ceased to fight.

questions.q: How did the Narayanastra treat soldiers who had thrown down their arms?
options: It marked them with burns but spared their lives
It scattered them far from the battlefield
It passed over them without doing any harm
It put them into a deathlike sleep until dawn
questions.explanation: The astra was harmless to anyone who had disarmed and surrendered even the inward wish to fight.

questions.q: What did Yudhishthira do in despair when the Narayanastra blazed forth?
options: He challenged Ashwatthama to single combat
He invoked the Pashupatastra in reply
He swore to fast unto death upon the field
He told the army to flee and gave way to hopelessness
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira despaired and urged the troops to save themselves before Krishna countermanded him and ordered everyone to disarm.

questions.q: By what principle did Bhima justify his defiance of the Narayanastra?
options: His vow to kill all hundred Kauravas
The kshatriya code that forbids laying down arms
His faith in the protection of Hanuman
Krishna's promise to guard the Pandavas
questions.explanation: Bhima declared that a kshatriya must never abandon his weapons in battle, and so the astra bore down on him alone.

questions.q: Which weapon did Ashwatthama hurl at Arjuna after the Narayanastra was spent?
options: Varunastra
Vajrastra
Agneyastra
Naga-pasha
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama next loosed the fiery Agneyastra, which scorched a division of the army before Arjuna neutralized it.

questions.q: What did the sky look like as the Narayanastra took effect?
options: It filled with discs, maces, and arrows blazing like the end of the world
It turned black as a moonless midnight
It split open to reveal Vishnu's heaven
It rained blood down upon both armies
questions.explanation: Witnesses saw the heavens crowded with countless burning weapons of every shape, multiplying against all resistance.

questions.q: Which astra did Arjuna invoke to try to shield Bhima from the Narayanastra's fire?
options: Brahmastra
Varunastra
Aindrastra
Pashupatastra
questions.explanation: Arjuna covered Bhima with the water-weapon Varunastra, and when even that failed he and Krishna dragged Bhima down bodily.

questions.q: According to Vyasa's consolation, why could the Narayanastra not destroy Krishna and Arjuna?
options: Their chariot bore Hanuman's protecting banner
Shiva had granted them immunity at Indrakila
They had secretly laid down their weapons first
They were Nara and Narayana, the weapon's own source
questions.explanation: Vyasa revealed that Krishna and Arjuna were the ancient sages Narayana and Nara, whom Vishnu's own weapon could not harm.

questions.q: What happened to the Narayanastra once the Pandava army surrendered?
options: It hung burning in the sky until sunset
It struck the earth and burned a vast crater
It turned back toward Ashwatthama's own host
It subsided, its storm of weapons melting away
questions.explanation: Finding no one offering resistance, the great astra grew calm and withdrew, leaving the disarmed army alive.

questions.q: In which section of the epic is the release of the Narayanastra described?
options: The Jayadratha-Vadha Parva
The Narayanastra-Mokshana Parva
The Samsaptaka-Vadha Parva
The Abhimanyu-Vadha Parva
questions.explanation: The episode gives its name to the Narayanastra-Mokshana Parva, a section within the Drona Parva.

questions.q: What determined the intensity of the Narayanastra's assault on any given warrior?
options: The measure of resistance he offered it
The number of sins he carried
His distance from the weapon's caster
The hour of the day at which it found him
questions.explanation: The astra grew fiercer against those who fought back, which is why total surrender was the only defense.

questions.q: After the Narayanastra failed, what doubt did Ashwatthama voice in his despair?
options: Whether his father had truly died by treachery
Whether Duryodhana deserved his loyalty
Why his infallible weapon had come to nothing against the Pandavas
Whether the gods had abandoned the Kaurava cause
questions.explanation: Shaken that Vishnu's own astra had failed, Ashwatthama questioned its futility until Vyasa explained the Nara-Narayana mystery.

questions.q: What did Krishna teach about warriors who resisted the Narayanastra only in their minds?
options: Thoughts were safe so long as hands were empty
Even a silent wish to fight drew the weapon's fury
Only spoken defiance angered the weapon
Prayer alone could turn the missiles aside
questions.explanation: Krishna warned that the astra struck anyone who harbored even the mental intent to fight, so the very wish for battle had to be surrendered.

questions.q: Alongside which weapons is the Narayanastra commonly ranked as a supreme astra?
options: The Gandiva and the Vijaya
The Vasavi Shakti and the Nagastra
The chakras of Indra and Yama
The Pashupatastra and the Brahmashirastra
questions.explanation: Tradition groups the great weapons of Vishnu, Shiva, and Brahma together as the deadliest astras in creation.
